meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no portfolio' trap by building a high-converting SaaS starter kit. Use Antigravity and Claude Code to ship professional apps at lightning speed.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is a developer's bottleneck. Solve it by building a modular SaaS starter kit that demonstrates authentication, database integration, and payment processing. By leveraging Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for rapid implementation, you can ship a production-ready foundation in hours, not weeks, to attract high-paying clients.
content.intro:
The silence of an empty portfolio is deafening. You have the skills, but the market demands proof—a tangible, scalable product that whispers 'hire me.' Most freelancers stall here, paralyzed by the complexity of building from scratch while trying to land their first big contract.

But the game has changed. With modern AI-augmented workflows, you no longer need to spend months coding boilerplate. You can engineer a sophisticated SaaS starter kit that serves as both your calling card and your engine for future client work. It is time to stop chasing experience and start manufacturing it.

steps.title: Define the Architecture
steps.description: Establish a clean, scalable directory structure that separates concerns, ensuring your starter kit is easily maintainable for future client hand-offs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a clean, modular directory structure for a Next.js SaaS starter kit including folders for components, hooks, services, and API routes. Focus on scalability and developer experience.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create the file structure based on the Antigravity architecture design.'

steps.title: Implement Auth and Database
steps.description: Integrate secure authentication and a robust database schema. This is the core 'trust' feature every client expects.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure authentication boilerplate using NextAuth and Prisma with a User model containing email, name, and subscription_status fields.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Install next-auth and prisma, then setup the schema.prisma file and auth provider.'

steps.title: Stripe Integration
steps.description: Add a payment gateway layer. Showing you can handle money is the fastest way to build authority.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe webhook handler and a checkout session API route for a monthly subscription model.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Install stripe sdk and create the API routes for checkout and webhook listening.'

steps.title: UI Component Library
steps.description: Build reusable UI components using Tailwind and Shadcn to ensure your future client projects look premium instantly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a set of standardized UI components: Button, Input, Card, and Navbar using Tailwind CSS and Radix UI primitives.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize shadcn/ui and add the core components specified in the prompt.'

steps.title: Deployment Pipeline
steps.description: Configure an automated CI/CD pipeline so your code deploys to Vercel with every git push.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Vercel deployment configuration file that includes environment variable validation and build caching.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure the vercel.json and ensure all environment variables are mapped for production.'

pro_tips: Always build with 'client-swappable' logic; keep your business logic separate from UI so you can re-skin the app for different niches.
Include a 'Demo Mode' toggle in your starter kit to allow prospects to test the functionality without creating a real account.
Document your code with JSDoc; clients value a codebase they can actually understand when you hand over the keys.
Use a 'Feature Flag' system early so you can turn off payment features for free-tier demos during sales calls.
Performance is a feature; use Lighthouse audits to prove your starter kit is faster than the competition.
content.next_steps: Deploy your starter kit to a custom domain, record a 60-second Loom video demonstrating the app's core flows, and add the project link to your LinkedIn and GitHub profile immediately.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the perfect project to fall into your lap. Create your own authority by building the tools that define your freelance career. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
